response: Bat, the amateur historian can determine the number of average purchases by first calculating the total value of the tokens issued. Since each token was worth 3 pence and 240 tokens were issued, the total value is 240 tokens × 3 pence/token = 720 pence. If the average purchase cost 18 pence, then the number of average purchases that could be made is 720 pence ÷ 18 pence/purchase = 40 purchases. Thus, the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year. P.P.S.: lake.
